programme.title: De Valera San Fhásach
programme.desc: Part one of two. Documentary about American-born Irish statesman and political leader Éamon de Valera, a leader in the 1916 Easter Rising. In 1924, De Valera was stripped of power and in poor mental health in prison. There was a cohort of radical women involved in the 1916 Rising and the War of Independence who supported him. Then the Treaty happened. As suddenly as he had gained power, de Valera was locked out of it. Opposing the Treaty gave these women common ground with De Valera, but who would end up the losers in this political game?

programme.title: The Eclipse
programme.desc: With Manue and Johanna off the investigation, David Levy orders a reconstruction. The teenagers re-enact the tragic event as the gendarmes watch on. Estranged since their children were accused, Manue and Johanna must put their disputes aside and join forces to discover the identity of Nours killer. programme.title: The Big Bang Theory

programme.title: This Farming Life

Nikki and Ollie welcome their first colony of rabbit kits as their breeding programme takes off. programme.title: This Farming Life
programme.desc: As the days grow longer, the MacDonalds round up their pregnant Highland cattle on the tiny island of Vallay, off North Uist, for a pre-natal check up and vitamin boost. Nikki and Ollie speculate to accumulate in Moray, splashing out on a new honesty shack.

programme.title: This Farming Life

Simon and Mary Sparrow live on a barge in Beccles, Suffolk, with their son Nate and their two cats. The Sparrows have been running a boatyard for nearly 10 years, specialising in refurbishing and renting out floating homes to holidaymakers. programme.title: My Floating Home

programme.title: Judge Judy
programme.desc: A single mother demands that her ex-lover pay her back for alleged loans to pay other outstanding loans and utility bills.

programme.title: Judge Judy
programme.desc: A mother is accused of treating her adult daughter like a baby when she stands up for her in court. Her daughter claims she was conned into paying more rent than was fair

programme.title: Judge Judy
programme.desc: Nine stitches are required after a man is hit in the head with a guitar; video evidence is submitted which shows the on-stage accident

programme.title: Judge Judy
programme.desc: Racial profiling during a routine traffic stop is the accusation laid against a veteran California cop who sues motorists for filling an allegedly false complaint against him

programme.title: Judge Judy
programme.desc: A man sues his teenage son for the return of a pet after his son decides to work at a cinema instead of going to college.

programme.title: Judge Judy
programme.desc: A woman fights to gain custody of her 11-year-old granddaughter; the childs mother accuses her of kidnapping.

programme.title: Judge Judy
programme.desc: A woman accuses a graphic designer of scamming her out of $1,000 after he allegedly failed to deliver a website.

programme.title: Forensic Factor
programme.desc: A series of seemingly random shootings have terrorised the city of East Point. Police are baffled as to the killers motive, and when he will strike again.

programme.title: 48 Hours
programme.sub-title: The Day My Mother Never Came Home
programme.desc: Reggie Reed has wrestled with the question of who murdered his mother decades ago. A fresh look at the evidence ultimately implicated Reeds father.

programme.title: Britains Almost Perfect Murders
programme.desc: Nurse Beverley Allitt murdered four children and harmed nine more in Lincolnshire, abusing trust until a pattern of deaths exposed her.

programme.title: Murder UK
programme.desc: Spoiled Elliot Turner, enraged when Emily Longley rejected him, murdered her in 2011 in Dorset. The investigation imprisoned Turner and convicted his parents for the cover up.

programme.title: Fatal Fraud
programme.desc: A couple set up a weapons business in West Virginia. All is not what it seems, leading to a fatal end.

programme.title: Cult Justice
programme.desc: Series exploring the magnetic power of criminal cult leaders. See how cult leader Jeffrey Lundgren ordered a series of ritualistic killings.

programme.title: Murder UK
programme.desc: Faria Khan hated her husband, Nawajid. Instead of divorce, she hired a group to execute a calculated, cold-blooded murder plot.

programme.title: Judge Judy
programme.desc: Ex-girlfriends fight over the alleged commercialisation of personal artwork intended to demonstrate acceptance and love during a major life transition.

programme.title: Judge Judy
programme.desc: A simple carpool to worship service ends in an auto accident and a hefty insurance settlement debate.

programme.title: Judge Judy
programme.desc: A woman airs her dirty laundry with the world when she discovers the father of her child is cheating on her.

programme.title: Judge Judy
programme.desc: A couple wait three years for a mans prison release to start their relationship, only to have it end with a heated assault.

programme.title: Judge Judy
programme.desc: A man sues his teenage son for the return of a pet after his son decides to work at a cinema instead of going to college.

programme.desc: A woman fights to gain custody of her 11-year-old granddaughter; the childs mother accuses her of kidnapping.

programme.title: Judge Judy
programme.desc: A woman accuses a graphic designer of scamming her out of $1,000 after he allegedly failed to deliver a website.

programme.title: Judge Judy
programme.desc: A reverend is sued for the return of money he was paid to gather signatures for an election ballot, many of which turned out to be invalid.

programme.title: Facing Evil
programme.desc: The case of Tyra Whitney, who killed her daughter Tashiras boyfriend LeMarcus Bell with a hammer in October 2010 after first blinding him with bleach. Whitney testified she was trying to defend her daughter from abuse

programme.title: Close Calls: On Camera
programme.desc: A family on a safari holiday are attacked by an elephant, while a singer and her band flee as a fire engulfs the five-star hotel at which they are performing. Nick Knowles presents

programme.title: Close Calls: On Camera
programme.desc: Nick Knowles presents more footage of serious incidents caught on CCTV and mobile phone. A supermarket manager and his girlfriend risk their own lives to save a pensioner from a burning car, a teenage adventurer has a near miss with an iceberg, and a family in Wales enjoying a day at the beach find a curious item, which turns out to be a bomb

programme.title: Judge Judy
programme.desc: An expecting mother allows her boyfriend to send risque photos of them together to his ex-girlfriend who allegedly punches her in the head.

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.sub-title: The Two Deaths of Hercule Flambeau
programme.desc: Following reports of Flambeaus death, the clerical sleuth races to protect a crown that contains a nail from the crucifixion of Christ. Mark Williams stars

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: The Caribbean-set whodunit returns for its 10th anniversary series, which promises more murders and the return of one or two familiar faces. A TV news presenter is found dead in her pool at home. DI Neville Parker is convinced her co-host is responsible. But given he was live on air at the time, how could he have done it? The return of an experienced sergeant to the island could be just what Neville needs to crack the case

programme.title: Murdoch Mysteries
programme.desc: The detective investigates when a wine connoisseur is poisoned at his sons wedding after threatening to expose a case of fraud

programme.title: Murdoch Mysteries
programme.desc: A recording device made by Alexander Graham Bell is used to assist in the investigation of a murder at a dinner honouring Helen Keller that was held in total darkness

programme.title: Miss Scarlet and the Duke

programme.title: Miss Scarlet and the Duke
programme.desc: Eliza is hired by a Death Photographer who is receiving bizarre and threatening messages from beyond the grave, and later makes a surprising discovery about her late father

programme.title: Inspector George Gently
programme.desc: An elderly man is killed in his dilapidated mansion, prompting Gently and Bacchus to delve into the buildings past as a childrens home. They soon have their prime suspect - a man who once lived there - but it becomes clear that some of the locals, including property developer Cora Davidson, have something to hide. Feature-length drama, guest starring Matthew McNulty and Jill Halfpenny, with Martin Shaw and Lee Ingleby

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: Flambeau is stunned to find his estranged father at St Marys, who has a dangerous mission in mind. Fifties-set crime drama, starring Mark Williams and John Light

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: The Catholic priest must discover the culprit when a novelist is attacked at a crime-writing festival. Fifties-set crime drama based on the stories by GK Chesterton, starring Mark Williams, with Amanda Mealing

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: Lady Felicia asks the ecclesiastical sleuth to help prove a convicted serial killer didnt kill her beloved goddaughter. Fifties-set crime drama based on the stories by GK Chesterton, starring Mark Williams and Nancy Carroll

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: When Gillian Fletcham reveals a talent scout is heading to Kembleford to find dancers for TV sensation Go Dancing, Mrs Devine and Brenda are delighted. As the showcase nears, Father Brown is shocked at how cut throat the world of competitive dancing is. Gillian dumps her regular dance partner, her brother Derek Fletcham in favour of Italian Stallion Mario Cantoloni, while award-winning couple Pauline Danbury and Robert Danbury find the pressure a strain on their relationship. The very next day, Gillian is found murdered at the dance school. Derek is firmly in the frame, but Father Brown isnt convinced and investigates the dancing rivalries. Fifties-set crime drama, guest starring Kevin Clifton, with Mark Williams

programme.title: Shakespeare and Hathaway: Private Investigators
programme.desc: Long-buried secrets are unearthed when Frank and Lu come to the aid of a community of allotment holders who are being threatened with eviction

programme.title: Shakespeare and Hathaway: Private Investigators
programme.desc: A carpet magnate is killed in an accident at his warehouse, but his youngest daughter suspects foul play and hires Frank and Luella to investigate her own two sisters

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: Lady Felicias impromptu visit coincides with Father Brown helping out a friend at an exclusive gentlemens club. Mystery drama based on the stories by GK Chesterton, starring Mark Williams and Nancy Carroll

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: An archaeologist is poisoned during a dig, and with only two suspects and a quick confession, it looks like this is going to be an open-and-shut case. However, it turns out the archaeologists were not alone - and the team realise they have little to no evidence. Meanwhile, Neville and Florence realise they might get along better than they originally thought, although the DIs attempts to embrace island life do not find favour with the Commissioner

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Rob Brydon hosts the comedy panel show in which two teams of celebs try to call each others bluff with a few stories about themselves that may be the truth or may be out-and-out porkies. Joining team captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack are comedians Rhod Gilbert and Hal Cruttenden, interior designer and former Dragons Den investor Kelly Hoppen and TV host and number-cruncher Carol Vorderman

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Rapper MIST, comedian Jayde Adams, interior designer Laurence Llewelyn-Bowen and former Paralympian swimmer Ellie Simmonds join host Rob Brydon and regular captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack on the offbeat panel show. As usual, the participants set out to hoodwink their opponents with absurd facts and plausible fibs about themselves in a bid to secure a team victory, through rounds such as Home Truths and Quick-fire Lies

programme.title: Wilderness with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: Simon travels across the Kalahari desert in southern Africa, one of the continents last great unspoilt wildernesses. He begins his journey in a small village in Namibias Nyae Nyae conservancy, where he sets out on a hunt for wildebeest with San trackers, who still live in the traditional way. programme.desc: DNA tests on a suspected robber prove his innocence but also reveal his father was an unnamed vagrant strangled on a tube train 15 years earlier. programme.title: Whitechapel
programme.desc: The crime thriller returns with the first of a two-part story, starring Rupert Penry-Jones. Chandler, Miles and the team investigate the murder of a man crushed to death by stones. As the killers macabre methods become clearer, a second body is discovered and the detectives suspect the executions are being carried out by a witch hunter. However, their progress is impeded as links to 20th-century espionage attract attention from the security services. With Phil Davis and Steve Pemberton

programme.title: The Inspector Lynley Mysteries
programme.desc: Feature-length episode. When a doorkeeper at the House of Lords is murdered, Lynley and Havers believe the victims gambling debts must have been the motive. When a clerk also dies, evidence of a wider conspiracy involving stolen papers is uncovered. The detectives locate the missing documents, but realise they are being followed and must catch the killer before someone beats them to it

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: The veteran detectives investigate the murder of a palaeontologist at the Natural History Museum, discovering the victim was a respected but outspoken scientist with a knack for rubbing people up the wrong way. When it comes out he was strongly opposed to the museums sponsorship deal with a large fuel company, the cops soon have plenty of suspects. Amanda Redman stars, with guest appearances by Trevor Bannister, Lucy Brown and Natasha Little

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: Dr Turner is reunited with a couple whose daughter was born without limbs, and takes them to a specialist unit where she can be fitted for prosthetics. However, while the experience helps the girls mother to realise that she is not alone, it proves traumatic for the father, who blames his wife for his childs condition. Nurse Crane takes charge of Sister Winifreds driving lessons, and Trixie thinks Christopher is hiding something from her

programme.title: Sister Boniface Mysteries
programme.desc: Sister Boniface is called by DI Gillespie to the home of minister of defence Charles Streatham, where guest Mary Sparkes has been found dead at the bottom of a staircase. Quickly assessing Sparkes has been murdered by poisoning rather than by a fall, Gillespie and DS Livingstone have to identify the murderer among the persons in the house - one is reporter Ruth Penny, disguised as a maid and looking for a scoop

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: CID officers Lennox, Proctor and Stanton spend the night in a building reputed to be haunted as part of a covert customs operation. At first they dismiss the idea of ghosts as pure fantasy and keep each other amused by telling horror stories - but then a suspect they are monitoring suddenly disappears into thin air

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: Dr Turner is reunited with a couple whose daughter was born without limbs, and takes them to a specialist unit where she can be fitted for prosthetics. However, while the experience helps the girls mother to realise that she is not alone, it proves traumatic for the father, who blames his wife for his childs condition. Nurse Crane takes charge of Sister Winifreds driving lessons, and Trixie thinks Christopher is hiding something from her

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: Sister Julienne oversees the opening of a family planning clinic at the local community centre, leading mother-of-three Wilma Goddens to start taking the contraceptive pill behind her husbands back. Barbara is distraught when she discovers that her father has accepted a missionary posting in New Guinea for the next three years so will not be able to officiate at her wedding, while Violet suffers terribly with hot flushes, Christopher asks Trixie to meet his daughter, and Delias heartache may finally be over

programme.title: Catherine Cooksons The Wingless Bird
programme.desc: Feature-length melodrama set in 1913, about the romantic relationship between Agnes Conway, whose parents own a sweetshop, and Charles Farrier, son of an altogether grander family. Agnes faces an added dilemma when she finds her heart divided between Charles and his soldier brother Reginald. Starring Claire Skinner and Edward Atterton

programme.title: Sharpe
programme.desc: Sean Bean returns as Bernard Cornwells swashbuckling hero in a feature-length story. As Sharpe and Harper journey across India in 1818 with the beautiful Marie-Angelique Bonnet, they come across a ragtag army unit supervising the transport of a suspected murderer. However, when the group is attacked by a bandit, Sharpe must get everyone safely to the next garrison - little suspecting he has stumbled across an opium-trafficking ring. Daragh OMalley and Beatrice Rosen also star

programme.title: Sister Boniface Mysteries
programme.desc: Sister Boniface is called to examine the body of Hilary Sympson-Smythe, who has died mysteriously, her face covered in cold cream and a completed jigsaw with one piece missing. A lonely hearts connection to Ruth Pennys newspaper leads Gillespie and Livingstone to investigate the male respondents. Ruth believes there is another victim who died in identical circumstances

programme.title: Silent Witness
programme.desc: An ex-convict is found murdered in his home and suspicion immediately lands on a recently released child killer. Nikki and the Lyell team are introduced to forensic psychologist Sasha Blackburn, who works closely with those released from prison on licence, as both the victim and one of the suspects are clients of hers. The investigators soon learn that it is not just the minds of these young men that are fragile, but their freedom is too. Feature-length episode of the crime drama, guest starring Lyndsey Marshal and Niamh Cusack

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: The team reopens an investigation into the death of an elderly woman whose decision to leave her estate to her cats sparked a family feud. The subsequent sudden death of the felines, which means the estate reverts to her relatives, prompts a neighbour to insist the detectives get to the bottom of her mysterious demise. Drama, guest starring Eric Sykes, Lindsey Coulson and Victor McGuire

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: Sister Julienne oversees the opening of a family planning clinic at the local community centre, leading mother-of-three Wilma Goddens to start taking the contraceptive pill behind her husbands back. Barbara is distraught when she discovers that her father has accepted a missionary posting in New Guinea for the next three years so will not be able to officiate at her wedding, while Violet suffers terribly with hot flushes, Christopher asks Trixie to meet his daughter, and Delias heartache may finally be over

programme.title: Art Detectives
programme.desc: When a wine critic and connoisseur collapses and dies during a tasting, a broken champagne flute and a crushed glass vial are the only clues. Guest starring Patrick Robinson

programme.title: Birds of a Feather
programme.desc: Sharon makes a go of it at the cafe, but Tracey finds it hard to feel pleased for her. Meanwhile, Dorien becomes preoccupied with more romantic pursuits. Comedy, starring Pauline Quirke and Linda Robson

programme.title: The Bill
programme.sub-title: When the Snow Lay Round About
programme.desc: With only two days to go until Christmas, the officers are hoping for a silent night at Sun Hill

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Archie deals with a case of an arrogant medical students food poisoning, which he traces to an unlikely source. Jimmi worries that things are moving too quickly with Eva, while Vivien is on the hunt for a new driver to take her for lessons. Medical drama series, starring Matt Kennard

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Michelle makes Ruth wear a flattering top, but the receptionist knows all too well the consequences if Davey sees her. Jimmi tries to get out of giving Vivien a driving lesson, but his attempts to put her off only fire her enthusiasm, and two students disagree about the morning-after pill

programme.title: Expedition Volcano
programme.desc: Documentary following scientists studying some of the worlds most dangerous active volcanos in the Democratic Republic of Congo. The first of two films explores Nyiragongo, which has erupted twice in the past 50 years, most recently in 2002, when it devastated the nearby city of Goma. However, it lies in a region troubled by war and humanitarian crises, which has made study of the volcano difficult. Former Royal Marine Aldo Kane is given the task of ensuring the teams safety

programme.title: Expedition Volcano
programme.desc: Part two of two. The team takes a helicopter to the crater of an active volcano in the Democratic Republic of Congo that has rarely been studied. The mountain is surrounded by forests, which provide cover for armed militant groups, and the scientists will only have a few short hours to gather information. They then move on to the surrounding area, a dangerous landscape marked by craters from past eruptions

programme.title: Expedition Borneo
programme.desc: Deep in the heart of the island of Borneo is a lost world of mountains, jungles and ravines which hide a vast abundance of wildlife. Here a team of scientists, climbers and wildlife film-makers gather to explore this extraordinary habitat before it is too late, as felling the rainforest for tropical timber and converting the land to oil palm plantations threaten this extraordinary ecosystem

programme.title: Expedition Borneo
programme.desc: One team searches for elephants, but the unforgiving parasites and leeches of the jungle begin to take their toll. Elsewhere, the mountaineers become lost on a remote peak

programme.title: Expedition Borneo
programme.desc: The team continues its exploration of Borneo, an expedition aimed at tracking down and filming the animals that live there. The adventurers go in search of wild orang-utan and experience a close encounter with a giant python, and one team member is bitten by a coral snake

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson test-drives the Nissan Cube and the Renault Clio 182, James May examines the Jaguar XJS, while Richard Hammond investigates alternative ways of fuelling cars and puts the Cadillac CTS through its paces. Terry Wogan is the Star in a Reasonably Priced Car

programme.title: Bottom 2001: A*** Oddity Live
programme.desc: Rik Mayall and Adrian Edmondson star in a stage show based on the comedy series, in which toilet humour is rife as the duo find themselves stranded on a Pacific island

programme.title: Gavin and Stacey
programme.desc: Bryn is in his element as he awaits the arrival of the Essex boys, who are having a big night out in Cardiff and staying for a sleepover, but Smithy is concerned about seeing Nessa again after their last meeting. Meanwhile, Stacey tells Gavin about a worrying discovery, and Pam and Mick discuss their plans for a new conservatory

programme.title: Gavin and Stacey
programme.desc: Nessa goes into labour a month early in Barry, and the newlyweds must forget their differences to track down Smithy in time for the birth of his child. Meanwhile, Stacey returns to Essex to salvage her marriage

programme.title: The Vicar of Dibley
programme.desc: Geraldine tries to make the supreme sacrifice by giving up chocolate for Lent. Mrs Cropleys also being good, vowing to steer clear of lard and fishpaste pancakes, and even Jim has a bash at self-denial. Meanwhile, Alices unwavering belief in the Dibley Bunny is ridiculed. Easter special from 1996

programme.title: Ghosts
programme.desc: When Mike spends his first night alone in the house with the ghosts, he assures Alison she should go out and let her hair down. The ghosts happily pass the time with a music club, but they are interrupted by some uninvited guests and it becomes their mission to try to alert Mike and figure out how to protect the house from the intruders.

programme.title: Ghosts
programme.desc: Heavy snow disrupts plans for the wedding as travel to Button House becomes near impossible, so Alison and Mike have to rally round to make sure Sam and Clares big day goes ahead. The Captain is in his element with the emergency preparations to the house, but Pat is distracted by an unexpected reminder of his past, while Lady Button is aghast at what Alison is allowing to take place at her former home

programme.title: Wallace and Gromit: The Wrong Trousers
programme.desc: Nick Parks Oscar-winning animation featuring the bumbling inventor and his faithful dog, whose lives are turned upside down after foolishly offering board and lodgings to villainous penguin Feathers McGraw. With the voice of Peter Sallis

programme.title: Only Fools and Horses
programme.desc: Del seizes a rare opportunity to make a quick profit and bring Trotters Independent Traders into the hi-tech world of satellite communications. Meanwhile, Rodney plunges to the depths of despair as his marriage continues to deteriorate. Comedy, starring David Jason and Nicholas Lyndhurst

programme.title: Only Fools and Horses
programme.desc: An audition gives Raquel the chance of a lifetime - and perhaps more importantly, an excuse to get out of Nelson Mandela House. Del-Boy is as excited as she is about the prospect, but only because he sees himself as the man behind her rapid rise to stardom. Comedy, starring David Jason, Nicholas Lyndhurst and Tessa Peake-Jones

programme.title: Only Fools and Horses
programme.desc: Del becomes a showbiz entrepreneur after discovering a sensational Tom Jones-style singer at a local club. Teaming him up with Raquel, he wastes no time in booking the duo at a birthday party for the elderly mother of a local hoodlum - but it soon becomes clear his new signing is not everything he is cracked up to be. Comedy, starring David Jason, Nicholas Lyndhurst and Tessa Peake-Jones, with a guest appearance by Philip Pope

programme.title: Only Fools and Horses
programme.desc: Del is invited to a school reunion at the Nags Head organised by old adversary Roy Slater (Jim Broadbent), who has recently been released from prison and claims to have turned over a new leaf. Comedy, starring David Jason, Nicholas Lyndhurst and Tessa Peake-Jones

programme.title: Only Fools and Horses
programme.sub-title: He Aint Heavy, Hes My Uncle
programme.desc: Uncle Albert falls victim to muggers, so Del-Boy resolves to take the law into his own hands, while Rodney becomes a director in Trotters Independent Traders - and his first job is to give the car a good clean. Comedy, starring David Jason, Nicholas Lyndhurst and Buster Merryfield

programme.title: Only Fools and Horses
programme.sub-title: Three Men, a Woman and a Baby
programme.desc: Rodney and Albert begin to worry the flat will get overcrowded as the birth of Del-Boy Jr approaches, while Del feels well prepared for fatherhood thanks to a stint at antenatal classes with Raquel. Starring David Jason and Nicholas Lyndhurst

programme.desc: Uncle Albert falls victim to muggers, so Del-Boy resolves to take the law into his own hands, while Rodney becomes a director in Trotters Independent Traders - and his first job is to give the car a good clean. Comedy, starring David Jason, Nicholas Lyndhurst and Buster Merryfield

programme.title: Only Fools and Horses
programme.sub-title: Three Men, a Woman and a Baby
programme.desc: Rodney and Albert begin to worry the flat will get overcrowded as the birth of Del-Boy Jr approaches, while Del feels well prepared for fatherhood thanks to a stint at antenatal classes with Raquel. Starring David Jason and Nicholas Lyndhurst

programme.title: Gavin and Stacey
programme.desc: The couple announce their engagement, prompting the Shipmans to throw a party in Billericay. Even Bryns decision to use his sat-nav for the journey fails to dampen their spirits, and everything goes swimmingly until the conversation turns to money - and the sweethearts drop a bombshell. Mathew Horne and Joanna Page star

programme.title: Gavin and Stacey
programme.desc: Everyone gathers in Billericay as Pete and Dawn prepare to renew their wedding vows. Gavin feels uncomfortable about Gwen and Bryn knowing the details of his sex life, and Dave hears about the shocking events of the curry night. Meanwhile, Smithy goes beyond the call of friendship when he makes a staggering suggestion

programme.title: Only Fools and Horses
programme.desc: Del-Boy becomes a yuppie, complete with red braces, Filofax and chrome briefcase. Meanwhile, Rodney begins attending a computer course at night school, where he meets Cassandra. Comedy, starring David Jason and Nicholas Lyndhurst

programme.title: The Vicar of Dibley
programme.desc: A funeral for a pet bird inspires Geraldine to transform the church into an ark and hold a special service for the animals of the village - but David is far from impressed by her stunt. Comedy, starring Dawn French and Gary Waldhorn

programme.title: Only Fools and Horses
programme.desc: Dodgy dealer Del gets his hands on a consignment of self-inflating rubber dolls, which turn out to be filled with explosive gas. Comedy, starring David Jason, Nicholas Lyndhurst and Buster Merryfield

programme.title: Only Fools and Horses
programme.desc: Dels beloved mum apparently makes contact during a seance at the Nags Head, inspiring him to visit the doctor to seek treatment for stomach cramps. Admitted to hospital for tests, he fears there is something seriously wrong with him - but a bacon sandwich and an alcoholic drink smuggled in by Rodney soon put the smile back on his face. Comedy, with David Jason, Nicholas Lyndhurst and Buster Merryfield

programme.title: Only Fools and Horses
programme.desc: A retired jeweller offers Del-Boy the deal of a lifetime - then suddenly has a heart attack and gets taken to hospital with a briefcase containing 250 cut-price gold necklaces still chained to his wrist. Comedy, starring David Jason and Nicholas Lyndhurst

programme.title: Only Fools and Horses
programme.desc: Del enters one of Rodneys old paintings in a competition and is over the moon when he wins a holiday for three at a five-star hotel in Majorca. Soon after, the Trotter brothers and Cassandra arrive at Palma Airport - but Del-Boy reveals there is a slight snag. Comedy, starring David Jason, Nicholas Lyndhurst and Gwyneth Strong

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: The Vicar of Dibley
programme.desc: First episode of Richard Curtiss comedy, starring Dawn French as the new vicar of a sleepy English village. Her arrival causes consternation among the locals - particularly stuffy councillor David Horton, who vows to have her ejected at all costs. Gary Waldhorn, James Fleet and Emma Chambers co-star

programme.title: The Vicar of Dibley
programme.desc: The Songs of Praise crew visits Dibley to record a programme in the village church. However, when Geraldine develops a crush on the producer her wayward emotions threaten to plunge the proceedings into disaster. Comedy, guest starring Peter Capaldi (The Thick of It)

programme.title: The Vicar of Dibley
programme.desc: Geraldine is keen to find a celebrity to open the autumn fair in style - and Alice inadvertently comes up with the ideal candidate by letting slip she is related to one Reg Dwight - AKA Elton John. Comedy, starring Dawn French, and Emma Chambers with a guest appearance by Kylie Minogue. First shown in 1994

programme.title: The Vicar of Dibley
programme.desc: A violent storm destroys St Barnabass stained glass window, and Geraldine is put in charge of raising funds to replace it - but the others are unsure whether any of her outlandish ideas will actually work. Comedy, starring Dawn French, Gary Waldhorn, James Fleet, Emma Chambers and Liz Smith

programme.title: The Vicar of Dibley
programme.desc: Local election fever hits the village and Geraldine finds she is a more popular candidate with the voters than over-confident parish council chairman David. Richard Curtiss comedy, starring Dawn French and Gary Waldhorn

programme.title: Only Fools and Horses
programme.desc: Rodney ties the knot with Cassandra, but Del has more on his mind than best man duties as the dreaded Driscoll brothers arrive at the Nags Head baying for his blood over some stolen mobile phones. Comedy, starring David Jason, Nicholas Lyndhurst and Gwyneth Strong

programme.title: Dads Army
programme.sub-title: Is There Honey Still for Tea?
programme.desc: Private Godfrey discovers the War Office is planning to demolish his cottage to make way for a new airstrip. Vintage comedy, starring Arthur Lowe, John Le Mesurier and Arnold Ridley. First shown in 1975

programme.title: Gavin and Stacey
programme.desc: The Shipmans drive to Wales for the wedding fayre, and after a momentous trip to the church for the reading of the banns, Nessa tells Stacey her big secret. Comedy, starring Mathew Horne and Joanna Page

programme.title: Gavin and Stacey
programme.desc: Gavin starts his job in Cardiff and, while Stacey is thrilled to be back in Wales, Smithy has to come to terms with living in Essex without his best friend. Nessa has taken up residence in Daves caravan in Sully and is getting ready for Neils christening. The event means a big reunion for the gang, at which Smithy receives surprising news from Nessa, and Stacey has a special request for her husband

programme.title: The Vicar of Dibley
programme.desc: Local election fever hits the village and Geraldine finds she is a more popular candidate with the voters than over-confident parish council chairman David. Richard Curtiss comedy, starring Dawn French and Gary Waldhorn

programme.title: Harry Enfield and Chums
programme.desc: More expertly crafted comedy sketches from Harry Enfield and colleagues-in-mirth Paul Whitehouse and Kathy Burke, featuring the Camp Jockeys, the Scousers and Kevin the teenager

programme.title: Gavin and Stacey
programme.desc: Smithy accompanies Nessa to the hospital for her first ultrasound scan, and the pair catch a glimpse of their baby. Stacey becomes unusually excited at the prospect of a weekend visit to her family home after she struggles to adjust to her role as an Essex wife - which proves to lack some of the glamour she had expected

programme.title: Only Fools and Horses
programme.desc: Rodney ties the knot with Cassandra, but Del has more on his mind than best man duties as the dreaded Driscoll brothers arrive at the Nags Head baying for his blood over some stolen mobile phones. Comedy, starring David Jason, Nicholas Lyndhurst and Gwyneth Strong

programme.title: Dont Wait Up
programme.desc: Toby has a problem with one of his patients, while Tom cannot afford the house he wants and has difficulty with an insurance medical. Nigel Havers and Tony Britton star

programme.title: Porridge
programme.desc: Peace evades the inmates of Slade Prison, and Fletchers bid to find a little tranquillity seems destined to fail. Comedy, starring Ronnie Barker and Brian Wilde

programme.title: Gavin and Stacey
programme.desc: Bryn is in his element as he awaits the arrival of the Essex boys, who are having a big night out in Cardiff and staying for a sleepover, but Smithy is concerned about seeing Nessa again after their last meeting. Meanwhile, Stacey tells Gavin about a worrying discovery, and Pam and Mick discuss their plans for a new conservatory

programme.title: Gavin and Stacey
programme.desc: Nessa goes into labour a month early in Barry, and the newlyweds must forget their differences to track down Smithy in time for the birth of his child. Meanwhile, Stacey returns to Essex to salvage her marriage

programme.title: The Vicar of Dibley
programme.desc: Geraldine tries to make the supreme sacrifice by giving up chocolate for Lent. Mrs Cropleys also being good, vowing to steer clear of lard and fishpaste pancakes, and even Jim has a bash at self-denial. Meanwhile, Alices unwavering belief in the Dibley Bunny is ridiculed. Easter special from 1996

programme.title: Ghosts
programme.desc: When Mike spends his first night alone in the house with the ghosts, he assures Alison she should go out and let her hair down. The ghosts happily pass the time with a music club, but they are interrupted by some uninvited guests and it becomes their mission to try to alert Mike and figure out how to protect the house from the intruders.

programme.title: Ghosts
programme.desc: Heavy snow disrupts plans for the wedding as travel to Button House becomes near impossible, so Alison and Mike have to rally round to make sure Sam and Clares big day goes ahead. The Captain is in his element with the emergency preparations to the house, but Pat is distracted by an unexpected reminder of his past, while Lady Button is aghast at what Alison is allowing to take place at her former home

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Wallace and Gromit: The Wrong Trousers
programme.desc: Nick Parks Oscar-winning animation featuring the bumbling inventor and his faithful dog, whose lives are turned upside down after foolishly offering board and lodgings to villainous penguin Feathers McGraw. With the voice of Peter Sallis

programme.title: Only Fools and Horses
programme.desc: Del seizes a rare opportunity to make a quick profit and bring Trotters Independent Traders into the hi-tech world of satellite communications. Meanwhile, Rodney plunges to the depths of despair as his marriage continues to deteriorate. Comedy, starring David Jason and Nicholas Lyndhurst

programme.title: Only Fools and Horses
programme.desc: An audition gives Raquel the chance of a lifetime - and perhaps more importantly, an excuse to get out of Nelson Mandela House. Del-Boy is as excited as she is about the prospect, but only because he sees himself as the man behind her rapid rise to stardom. Comedy, starring David Jason, Nicholas Lyndhurst and Tessa Peake-Jones

programme.title: Only Fools and Horses
programme.desc: Del becomes a showbiz entrepreneur after discovering a sensational Tom Jones-style singer at a local club. Teaming him up with Raquel, he wastes no time in booking the duo at a birthday party for the elderly mother of a local hoodlum - but it soon becomes clear his new signing is not everything he is cracked up to be. Comedy, starring David Jason, Nicholas Lyndhurst and Tessa Peake-Jones, with a guest appearance by Philip Pope

programme.title: Only Fools and Horses
programme.desc: Del is invited to a school reunion at the Nags Head organised by old adversary Roy Slater (Jim Broadbent), who has recently been released from prison and claims to have turned over a new leaf. Comedy, starring David Jason, Nicholas Lyndhurst and Tessa Peake-Jones

programme.desc: Bank Holiday Monday sees the Essex contingent arrive for a sunny day at the beach in Barry. Mathew Horne, Joanna Page, James Corden and Ruth Jones star

programme.desc: Tony feels depressed thanks to his ever-expanding waistline and Garys secret stash of cash. Comedy, starring Martin Clunes, Neil Morrissey, Leslie Ash and Caroline Quentin

programme.title: Are You Being Served?
programme.sub-title: Diamonds Are a Mans Best Friend
programme.desc: A wealthy customer loses a valuable diamond in the store and offers a reward for its return. Shop-floor comedy, starring Trevor Bannister, John Inman and Frank Thornton

programme.title: Louis Theroux: Miami Mega Jail
programme.desc: Part one of two. The contemplative reporter examines life in Miami County Jail, where most of the inmates are awaiting trial. He begins by exploring the large cage-like dwellings on the fifth and sixth floors, which are home to many of the most volatile prisoners. The inmates lives are governed by a gladiatorial code, leading to fights for food and status - a situation the guards say they are powerless to address

programme.title: Louis Theroux: Miami Mega Jail
programme.desc: Part two of two. The reporter goes deeper into the jail system, meeting an alleged triple murderer facing a possible death sentence. He also follows a group of younger convicts who have avoided prison by pleading guilty and attending a four-month military-style boot camp. Among them is a 14-year-old boy who may face a 10-year sentence for armed robbery if he cannot endure the harsh training and rehabilitation programme

programme.title: Inside the Ambulance
programme.desc: An asthmatic deteriorates rapidly in the ambulance, and the crews are called to a 30-year-old car thats been driven into a wall by a 90-year-old driver

programme.title: Inside the Ambulance
programme.desc: The West Midlands crews treat a pensioner whose heart rate is so erratic they are worried he might have a heart attack on the way to the hospital

programme.title: Inside the Ambulance
programme.desc: Paramedics blue light it to a man who had a bad fall and has a suspected spinal injury, while Ash and Craig help a 12-year-old boy who has been attacked at his local fun fair

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Part two of two. The babys body is found and evidence stacks up against the nannys boyfriend, but Sams gentle interrogation of the parents produces another startling suspect. Eventually, toxicology reports on the victim present a stark truth demanding all the DIs sensitivity and tact as she helps the grieving mother face up to what happened

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Part one of two. When a talented young athlete is found battered to death in a park, officers interrogate fellow guests at a house party he had attended earlier that evening. His schoolmates suggest gatecrashers bringing drugs into the house may have been responsible, but their versions of events reveal inconsistencies and suggest a completely different explanation

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Part two of two. Heaton and Meadows play a psychological game, putting pressure on the teenage partygoers by staging a reconstruction and holding a press conference to rattle the composure of the suspects - aiming to uncover the truth about the death of the young athlete. Eddie finds a piece of wood which he believes is the murder weapon, but the nail that caused the puncture wound in the victims spine is missing

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Part one of three. Human remains found buried in a park turn out to be those of a student who went missing 20 years previously. Suspicion immediately falls on her boyfriend and a couple they were close to in their teens. The case brings Heaton face to face with an old adversary, raising the possibility that the man he sent down for murder in the 1980s may in fact be a serial killer

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Part two of three. The discovery of two more bodies convinces Heaton his suspect is a serial killer, but the man he sent to prison 20 years before sticks to his story that he is innocent. A retired colleague who worked on the original case reveals that vital evidence was withheld at the time of the trial and the pressure mounts to re-examine the prosecution. Bad Girls James Gaddas guest stars

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts the quiz in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts the quiz in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: 999 Rescue Squad
programme.desc: A suspected acid attack on the streets of Bradford leads to a busy shift for the crew. Documentary following the work of Yorkshire Ambulance Services Hazardous Area Response Team

programme.title: 999 Rescue Squad
programme.desc: A police chase ends with a spectacular crash and five trapped teens in the wreckage

programme.title: 999 Rescue Squad
programme.desc: The team races to save an injured woman trapped in a van crushed between two lorries

programme.title: 999 Rescue Squad
programme.desc: Featuring a stand-off involving a man wielding a samurai sword in South Yorkshire

programme.title: Nurses on the Ward
programme.desc: Amy deals with a serious emergency during a birth, Caroline must rapidly help her patient breathe again, and Rhona treats Keith, who may have a life-threatening infection

programme.title: Nurses on the Ward
programme.desc: Three young newly-qualified twenty-something nurses Renee, Caitlin and Military Nurse James cope with the pressures of working for the NHS and finding a work-life balance

programme.title: Louis Theroux: Shooting Joe Exotic
programme.desc: Louis revisits his 2011 documentary Americas Most Dangerous Pets, returning to Oklahoma to explore what has happened since. Joe Exotic, aka Joseph Allen Maldonado-Passage, now resides in a federal prison after being found guilty of a murder-for-hire plot and multiple animal cruelty charges, and having been the subject of hit Netflix documentary Tiger King

programme.title: The Young Midwives of Essex
programme.sub-title: With Great Power Comes Great Responsibility
programme.desc: In the final episode of the series, Tacha and Lilli must make decisions about their futures. The young midwives enjoy a spa day

programme.title: Wagspiracy: Vardy v Rooney
programme.desc: With access to key individuals who didnt give evidence at the trial, this documentary explores the hidden workings of the tabloids, PRs and agents connected to so-called Wagatha Christie, which followed accuisations between footballers wives Coleen Rooney and Rebekah Vardy. Contributors include Colin Paterson and Lizzie Cundy

programme.title: Louis Therouxs LA Stories
programme.desc: California has some of the toughest regulations controlling sex offenders anywhere in America - after leaving prison they can spend years on parole being tagged and tracked and a lifetime on an online register. In his final film exploring different aspects of life in Los Angeles, Louis Theroux visits hostels to talk to parolees as well as the people charged with monitoring them, and asks whether the restrictions keep society safer and when, if ever, it is right to give sex offenders a second chance

programme.title: Inside the Ambulance
programme.desc: Crews from the West Midlands attend a diabetic who has collapsed at home, reassure the wife of a man with heart problems and keep up the spirits of a woman with womb cancer

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: My Family
programme.desc: Abi tries to cook a meal for her future mother-in-law and discovers she is not prepared to give up Roger without a fight. Ben looks forward to a visit from an old college pal, only to find out Charlie is not the man he used to be. Comedy, starring Robert Lindsay and Zoe Wanamaker

programme.title: My Family
programme.desc: Roger and Abis wedding day arrives, but the event does not go off without a glitch. Ben dispenses advice on what makes a happy marriage, leaving the bride with serious doubts in her mind, while Janey is dumped by her latest boyfriend and turns up at the church already inebriated

programme.title: My Family
programme.sub-title: Breaking Up Aint Hard to Do
programme.desc: Susan learns two of her old friends are having marital problems, and in an effort to help invites them round for dinner. However, a blazing row soon escalates between the troubled couple - which in turn causes friction in the Harpers own marriage

programme.title: My Family
programme.desc: Susans severe mood swings lead Michael to believe she is going through the menopause, and his suspicions are only confirmed when she is arrested for stealing a pair of sunglasses. Ben learns the cost of lawyers and decides to represent her in court, but his line of defence proves weak

programme.title: Bad Girls Behind Bars
programme.desc: The segregation pod of Western Massachusetts Regional Womens Correctional Centre houses the most violent inmates. When one of them attacks an officer, it takes all available staff to restrain her, and Sergeant Clayton begins an investigation into the reasons for the assault. Two prisoners who have been separated conduct a secret relationship, and a Satanist struggles to practice her religion behind bars

programme.title: My Family
programme.desc: Ben falls in love with his neighbours plasma-screen TV when he takes delivery of it for them. Meanwhile, Michaels current flame may be expecting a smaller but more problematic delivery. Domestic comedy, starring Robert Lindsay and Zoe Wanamaker as harassed parents

programme.title: My Family
programme.desc: Bens attempt to pay money into his bank is thwarted when someone else tries to make a withdrawal - with the aid of sawn-off shotguns. Things look bleak for the hostages when he decides to act as a negotiator. Robert Lindsay stars, with a guest appearance by Pauline Quirke

programme.title: Bangers and Cash: Restoring Classics
programme.desc: The team break with tradition and actually follow Dave Mathewsons advice on what to restore - he points to a 1992 Vauxhall Nova Merit Plus!

programme.title: Retro Electro Workshop
programme.desc: Shamil hunts down a battered 1940s Marconi Radiogram that has seen better days, and Rob is tasked with getting a 50s TV working again by the family who made them

programme.title: Retro Electro Workshop
programme.desc: Shamil buys an 80s Technics record deck for Rob to restore and Rob is on a call out to a museum where a Marconi set needs repair.

programme.title: Masters of Reinvention
programme.desc: The team reinvent a 1936 design for a three-wheeled car using recycled parts and a reclaimed recumbent trike. Plus, Ruth and Carl rework a vintage burglar alarm

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo journeys from Piccadilly Circus to Gravesend, visiting an exotic West End store that was popular among Victorian women and hearing how a Russian ballet company took London by storm in 1909. He also discovers the origins of netball and gymslips at North Kent College and meets the pilots of the Port of London Authority, founded in the early 20th century

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo heads from Warwick to Rye in East Sussex. In the orchards of East Malling, Kent, Michael discovers how the Edwardians serious attitude towards cultivation bears fruit even now. In Folkestone, he hears how the town coped with an influx of more than 100,000 refugees fleeing the German invasion in 1914. In the High Weald, Michael heads for Tenterden Town and the light railway that opened in 1900. His final stop is the medieval Cinque Port of Rye, where he tours the home and garden of author Henry James

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo explores the Britain of the 1930s as he sets out on another adventure. Beginning just outside Newcastle in Jarrow, Michael uncovers the desperation that led 200 men to march 300 miles to Westminster in order to petition the government for work. The first leg of his journey ends Spennymoor, Co Durham, where he meets the son of a miner who became one of the most famous 20th-century artists of the region

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo is in Northumbria en route to the Highlands, heading to Kielder Forest to discover what lay behind a national initiative to plant one of the largest manmade woodlands in Europe. Crossing the border to Scotland, Michael arrives in the weaving town of Hawick to visit Lovat Mill, where, in the 1930s, tweed was big business, and also goes to the movies, sitting in the front stalls at the Dominion cinema in Morningside

programme.title: Restoration Workshop
programme.desc: Antiques dealer and expert restorer Gary Wallis and his team of highly skilled craftspeople turn the tired and unloved items into objects that will turn a large profit. In the opening episode, they restore a Grenadier guards drum, create a garden feature from a helicopter tripod and re-purpose a vintage car grille into a gleaming design classic

programme.title: Restoration Workshop
programme.desc: Gary Wallis re-invents an old ships compass and a grand display cabinet into two of his most successful transformations to date

programme.title: Restoration Workshop
programme.desc: Gary Wallis visits an automobilia auction at Silverstone, creates a chair out of Second World War bomber jackets and gives a Victorian table a new zinc top

programme.title: Restoration Workshop
programme.desc: Gary Wallis visits Kempton Park Autojumble. He buys a tired vintage Moto Guzzi bike and transforms it into stunning display piece

programme.title: Allo Allo!
programme.desc: British troops save Edith and Rene from execution, and the pair are released into the woods. Meanwhile, the Germans decide to head for Spain, and Michelle joins Crabtree in a duet. Classic wartime sitcom, starring Gorden Kaye and Carmen Silvera

programme.title: Allo Allo!
programme.desc: The Colonel and Grubers escape plan has gone awry, so they try to make general Von Klinkerhoffen believe the Resistance had kidnapped them

programme.title: Allo Allo!
programme.desc: Rene is imprisoned by communist leader Louise, who plans to use him as her love slave, while Mimi, Edith and Yvette are convinced he is dead and decide to hold a seance

programme.title: Canal Boat Diaries
programme.desc: Robbie Cumming explores the English canal network, beginning with a journey from Shardlow to Etruria Junction, Stoke on Trent, exploring the Trent and Mersey Canal. However, not everything goes to plan, and he will have to deal with a tricky issue with his propeller as well as navigating the complicated locks on the Trent and Mersey Canal

programme.title: Canal Boat Diaries
programme.desc: Robbie Cumming explores life on the English canal network. A low canal tunnel in Staffordshire proves to be problematic and Robbie helps out a stranded boater

programme.title: Canal Boat Diaries
programme.desc: Robbie Cummings exploration of the English canal network continues across the Pennines from Marple to Huddersfield, where his journey is hampered by a fallen tree and a leaking lock pound

programme.title: Canal Boat Diaries
programme.desc: Robbie Cumming travels along the Rochdale Canal from Sowerby Bridge to Manchester. But disaster strikes when he runs aground, threatening to stop him from reaching his final destination

programme.title: Dom Chineas Cornish Workshop
programme.desc: Dom Chinea builds a drift car with engineer Sam Lovegrove. At home, he gets a digger in to make his new pond, and he and Sam have a go at gig rowing after fixing the club trailer in Hayle

programme.title: Tropic of Capricorn
programme.desc: Simon Reeve embarks on the third leg of his journey, crossing the vast expanse of mainland Australia. Heading east from Ningaloo Reef, the explorer comes across a ghost town in Wittenoom Gorge, contaminated beyond habitation by a nearby asbestos mine. Travelling across the Northern Territory, Simon witnesses the shocking living conditions endured by Aboriginal communities in the shadow of Uluru

programme.title: Tropic of Capricorn
programme.desc: On the final leg of his journey around the world, Simon Reeve follows the tropic across South America. In Argentina he meets an English anthropologist who married into a forest tribe. On the Paraguay-Brazil border he visits a hotbed of smuggling activity, and in Sao Paulo, the globetrotter takes in a neighbourhood formerly considered the most dangerous in the world

programme.title: David Jasons Great British Inventions
programme.desc: The actor examines British innovations in the history of engineering. In the first episode, David takes a look at wheeled vehicles from the Penny Farthing bicycle to Stephensons rocket - as well as being reunited with an original Reliant Regal Supervan, as used in Only Fools and Horses

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Jack has his eye on a Peugeot 205 GTI that could be one of the finest examples in the country. Plus, Derek collects a pair of sporty 60s Triumphs

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Derek and Paul are off to Leeds on a rare father and son road trip to pick up a VW Cabriolet and a Lotus Elite

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Derek navigates the dry stone walls of the Peak District to collect a very original 1988 Opel Manta. Plus, Dave deals with a Ford Granada rally support vehicle

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Paul is picking up a stunning 1961 Vauxhall Cresta, inspired by American design, the car changed the face of British motoring

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Derek has a three-hour drive in a 1960s bus that arrived in the UK after a life in the sun. Dave takes a Triumph TR7 convertible to auction

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Daves on his way to Grimsby to pick up an iconic 80s fast Ford, the Mk1 Fiesta XR2, while Bubbles regrets sitting in an immaculate Morris 1800 which hes now desperate to bid on

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Derek gets a tour of Hadlow Castle in Kent when he arrives to collect the owners pre-war Alvis and an immaculate Citroen used by the Gestapo in occupied France

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Following the route of the old Caledonian Railway Company, Michael Portillo discovers the Riviera of the Highlands. With a daily direct train service to Kings Cross, Gleneagles remains a top destination and is also headquarters for the British School of Falconry. Berry Town, aka Blairgowrie, is Michaels next stop. At the home of the Scottish raspberry industry, which once sent fruit to London daily on board a raspberry special service from Cooper Angus station, Michael learns how to pick this most delicate of berries. The last stop on this leg is the city of Dundee, where he visits publisher DC Thomson

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo continues his railway journey from Newcastle to Loch Ness, steered by his 1930s Bradshaws Guide. Stopping at Dundee, Michael heads for Glamis Castle, where Lady Elizabeth Bowes-Lyon, the Queen Mother, grew up. Michael hears about her happy childhood and how she later found sanctuary there, when King Edward VIII abdicated and she and Prince Albert unexpectedly became king and queen of the United Kingdom

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo is at Elgins port, the coastal town of Lossiemouth, where the Labour Partys first prime minister James Ramsay MacDonald was born. Nearby, Michael finds a remote boarding school established in the 1930s and famous today both for its unusual ideas and its royal former pupils. His penultimate stop is Inverness, where he uncovers the work of female photographer MEM Donaldson, who documented a Highland way of life that was rapidly disappearing. Michaels journey ends at Loch Ness, where he joins a Deep Scan research team scouring the deep for signs of the elusive monster

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo explores the West Country between the wars, setting off from the Cornish seaside resort of St Ives and ending his first in the former mining village of St Day. He visits Britains first studio pottery, where he attempts a decorative wax technique and feels the heat of the firing kiln. Michael also joins a family on holiday in a railway carriage called Harvey

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Jack has his eye on a Peugeot 205 GTI that could be one of the finest examples in the country. Plus, Derek collects a pair of sporty 60s Triumphs

programme.title: Retro Electro Workshop
programme.desc: Shamil hunts down a battered 1940s Marconi Radiogram that has seen better days, and Rob is tasked with getting a 50s TV working again by the family who made them

programme.desc: Shamil buys an 80s Technics record deck for Rob to restore and Rob is on a call out to a museum where a Marconi set needs repair.

Crossing the border to Scotland, Michael arrives in the weaving town of Hawick to visit Lovat Mill, where, in the 1930s, tweed was big business, and also goes to the movies, sitting in the front stalls at the Dominion cinema in Morningside

programme.title: Restoration Workshop
programme.desc: Antiques dealer and expert restorer Gary Wallis and his team of highly skilled craftspeople turn the tired and unloved items into objects that will turn a large profit. In the opening episode, they restore a Grenadier guards drum, create a garden feature from a helicopter tripod and re-purpose a vintage car grille into a gleaming design classic

programme.title: Restoration Workshop
programme.desc: Gary Wallis re-invents an old ships compass and a grand display cabinet into two of his most successful transformations to date

programme.title: Restoration Workshop
programme.desc: Gary Wallis visits an automobilia auction at Silverstone, creates a chair out of Second World War bomber jackets and gives a Victorian table a new zinc top

programme.title: Restoration Workshop
programme.desc: Gary Wallis visits Kempton Park Autojumble. He buys a tired vintage Moto Guzzi bike and transforms it into stunning display piece

programme.title: Allo Allo!
programme.desc: British troops save Edith and Rene from execution, and the pair are released into the woods. Meanwhile, the Germans decide to head for Spain, and Michelle joins Crabtree in a duet. Classic wartime sitcom, starring Gorden Kaye and Carmen Silvera

programme.title: Allo Allo!
programme.desc: The Colonel and Grubers escape plan has gone awry, so they try to make general Von Klinkerhoffen believe the Resistance had kidnapped them

programme.title: Allo Allo!
programme.desc: Rene is imprisoned by communist leader Louise, who plans to use him as her love slave, while Mimi, Edith and Yvette are convinced he is dead and decide to hold a seance

programme.title: Canal Boat Diaries
programme.desc: Robbie Cumming explores the English canal network, beginning with a journey from Shardlow to Etruria Junction, Stoke on Trent, exploring the Trent and Mersey Canal. However, not everything goes to plan, and he will have to deal with a tricky issue with his propeller as well as navigating the complicated locks on the Trent and Mersey Canal

programme.title: Canal Boat Diaries
programme.desc: Robbie Cumming explores life on the English canal network. A low canal tunnel in Staffordshire proves to be problematic and Robbie helps out a stranded boater

programme.title: Canal Boat Diaries
programme.desc: Robbie Cummings exploration of the English canal network continues across the Pennines from Marple to Huddersfield, where his journey is hampered by a fallen tree and a leaking lock pound

programme.title: Canal Boat Diaries
programme.desc: Robbie Cumming travels along the Rochdale Canal from Sowerby Bridge to Manchester. But disaster strikes when he runs aground, threatening to stop him from reaching his final destination

programme.title: Dom Chineas Cornish Workshop
programme.desc: Dom Chinea builds a drift car with engineer Sam Lovegrove. At home, he gets a digger in to make his new pond, and he and Sam have a go at gig rowing after fixing the club trailer in Hayle

programme.title: Tropic of Capricorn
programme.desc: Simon Reeve embarks on the third leg of his journey, crossing the vast expanse of mainland Australia. Heading east from Ningaloo Reef, the explorer comes across a ghost town in Wittenoom Gorge, contaminated beyond habitation by a nearby asbestos mine. Travelling across the Northern Territory, Simon witnesses the shocking living conditions endured by Aboriginal communities in the shadow of Uluru

programme.title: Tropic of Capricorn
programme.desc: On the final leg of his journey around the world, Simon Reeve follows the tropic across South America. In Argentina he meets an English anthropologist who married into a forest tribe. On the Paraguay-Brazil border he visits a hotbed of smuggling activity, and in Sao Paulo, the globetrotter takes in a neighbourhood formerly considered the most dangerous in the world

programme.title: David Jasons Great British Inventions
programme.desc: The actor examines British innovations in the history of engineering. In the first episode, David takes a look at wheeled vehicles from the Penny Farthing bicycle to Stephensons rocket - as well as being reunited with an original Reliant Regal Supervan, as used in Only Fools and Horses

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Jack has his eye on a Peugeot 205 GTI that could be one of the finest examples in the country. Plus, Derek collects a pair of sporty 60s Triumphs

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Derek and Paul are off to Leeds on a rare father and son road trip to pick up a VW Cabriolet and a Lotus Elite

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Derek navigates the dry stone walls of the Peak District to collect a very original 1988 Opel Manta. Plus, Dave deals with a Ford Granada rally support vehicle

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Derek has a three-hour drive in a 1960s bus that arrived in the UK after a life in the sun. Dave takes a Triumph TR7 convertible to auction

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Derek inspects a brace of old cop cars including a Rover P6 complete with radio, sirens and V8 engine, and a former Mod waves ciao to his collection of 1960s Italian scooters

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Daves on his way to Grimsby to pick up an iconic 80s fast Ford, the Mk1 Fiesta XR2, while Bubbles regrets sitting in an immaculate Morris 1800 which hes now desperate to bid on

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Derek gets a tour of Hadlow Castle in Kent when he arrives to collect the owners pre-war Alvis and an immaculate Citroen used by the Gestapo in occupied France

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Following the route of the old Caledonian Railway Company, Michael Portillo discovers the Riviera of the Highlands. With a daily direct train service to Kings Cross, Gleneagles remains a top destination and is also headquarters for the British School of Falconry. Berry Town, aka Blairgowrie, is Michaels next stop. At the home of the Scottish raspberry industry, which once sent fruit to London daily on board a raspberry special service from Cooper Angus station, Michael learns how to pick this most delicate of berries. The last stop on this leg is the city of Dundee, where he visits publisher DC Thomson

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo continues his railway journey from Newcastle to Loch Ness, steered by his 1930s Bradshaws Guide. Stopping at Dundee, Michael heads for Glamis Castle, where Lady Elizabeth Bowes-Lyon, the Queen Mother, grew up. Michael hears about her happy childhood and how she later found sanctuary there, when King Edward VIII abdicated and she and Prince Albert unexpectedly became king and queen of the United Kingdom

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo is at Elgins port, the coastal town of Lossiemouth, where the Labour Partys first prime minister James Ramsay MacDonald was born. Nearby, Michael finds a remote boarding school established in the 1930s and famous today both for its unusual ideas and its royal former pupils. His penultimate stop is Inverness, where he uncovers the work of female photographer MEM Donaldson, who documented a Highland way of life that was rapidly disappearing. Michaels journey ends at Loch Ness, where he joins a Deep Scan research team scouring the deep for signs of the elusive monster

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo explores the West Country between the wars, setting off from the Cornish seaside resort of St Ives and ending his first in the former mining village of St Day. He visits Britains first studio pottery, where he attempts a decorative wax technique and feels the heat of the firing kiln. Michael also joins a family on holiday in a railway carriage called Harvey

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Jack has his eye on a Peugeot 205 GTI that could be one of the finest examples in the country. Plus, Derek collects a pair of sporty 60s Triumphs

programme.title: Canal Boat Diaries
programme.desc: Robbie Cumming sets out on the last leg of his journey across central England. While crossing an epic aqueduct near Stratford-upon-Avon, the narrowboat enthusiast gets stuck in a lock in the heart of Birmingham. First broadcast 9 December 2020

programme.title: UEFA Man in the Middle
programme.desc: Following some of European footballs top officials as they adapt to the introduction of VAR, giving their frank assessments of some of the most-talked-about decisions

programme.title: Sport Stories
programme.desc: Part two of two. The former Republic of Ireland manager opens up about life as a manager, taking the Ireland job twice, the 2002 World Cup, and the infamous relationship with Roy Keane

programme.title: The Finish Line
programme.desc: Roman Kemp and Sarah Greene host this quiz contest, in which five contestants race in moving podiums across the studio floor to try and win £5,000. The contenders battle across a series of fast-paced qualifiers and head-to-head races where the aim of the game is simple - to make it to the finish line before their rivals. The winner then races against the track itself in an attempt to win the cash prize

programme.title: Wild Britain
programme.desc: Cameras follow some of the animals that thrive in inner-city environments across the country. Urban foxes did not exist in Britain until the 1930s, but now these animals inhabit 90% of towns and cities and take residence in built-up living quarters. Meanwhile, cameras observe families of hedgehogs thriving in unkempt gardens, and peregrine falcons nesting in some on Londons tallest buildings

programme.title: Paul OGrady: For the Love of Dogs
programme.desc: The comedian returns to Battersea Dogs and Cats Home in London, meeting Spencer the stray beagle, who is consigned to quarantine with a contagious virus and petrified of humans. Paul also meets Lydia, the Hungarian Puli, a heavily dreadlocked rare pedigree, who is struggling in the hot summer heat, and Tintin the tearaway Yorkshire terrier who he takes for a trip to the hairdressers

programme.title: Paul OGrady: For the Love of Dogs
programme.desc: The comedian meets three dogs in need of companionship. First up is excitable 10-month-old mongrel Blake, who loves playing with his toys - but when Paul suspects his eyesight is not as it should be and the vets confirm his fears, the presenter is given the challenge of finding the canine a suitable friend to act as his very own guide dog. Paul also meets Jacko the Jack Russell, who has been down in the dumps ever since arriving at Battersea, and 14-week-old Saluki Forest, who is in quarantine due to a stomach bug. Paul tries to come up with a way of keeping the puppy entertained - but with no physical contact allowed, that proves easier said than done

programme.title: The Royal
programme.desc: Dr Ormerod finally accepts that wife Caroline isnt going to emerge from her coma and decides to move on. A married couple face the truth about their future, and Dr Weatherill is forced to break a distressing diagnosis to a patient, while Kens organisational skills ensure the charity ball goes with a bang. Starring Michael Starke, Amy Robbins and Robert Daws, and featuring a guest appearance by Tricia Penrose in her Heartbeat guise of Gina Ward

programme.title: The Royal
programme.desc: A student nurse causes quite a stir when she arrives in the middle of the night, while Ken and Alun contend with a farming family suffering a crisis. Brigid and Jill deliver a Downs syndrome baby whose parents dont want anything to do with it, and Lizzies crash diet has alarming consequences. Starring Wendy Craig, Robert Daws and Amy Robbins

programme.desc: Part one of an interview with the pundit, activist, barrister and former footballer, who opens up about his life, trauma and controversies

programme.title: Paul OGrady: For the Love of Dogs
programme.desc: The dog-loving funnyman pays another visit to Battersea, where he falls for the toothless boxer Prince. The pair hit it off immediately, leaving Paul to wonder whether he should rehome the canine himself. But there is bad news - Prince has a suspicious lump on his leg that requires urgent attention. Paul also meets Yorkshire terrier Dash, whose bark has lost its bite leaving him with a very unusual, squeaky voice. As if that were not bad enough, the little canine soon earns the nickname Dash the Slash after the comedian takes him out for a walk - and ends up with a wet leg

programme.title: Paul OGrady: For the Love of Dogs
programme.desc: Paul meets Happi, a Nepalese mountain dog - a breed never featured on the show before - who is a lover of vegetarian food, putting the funnymans culinary skills to the test as he prepares the poochs dinner. He also befriends Rumble the greyhound, who has an unusual problem - he is so happy, his tail has become damaged with all the wagging it does. Finally, Paul hears the story of Parsnip and Carrot, both suspected victims of backstreet breeding. Parsnip in particular is in a bad way, with infected mammary glands as a result of a recent pregnancy

programme.title: The Royal
programme.desc: Tragedy strikes at the Royal v Ashfordly General rugby match, as Cheriton dives to score a try, only to be pounced on by opponents. It soon emerges that his injuries are serious, and Ormerod rushes to help his colleague - but his efforts prove futile and Cheriton dies at the scene. Meanwhile, Alun is involved in a road accident caused by a man who recently suffered a stroke, and student nurse Samantha Beaumont fails to impress Matron and Sister Brigid by putting pink sheets on the wards beds. Medical drama, starring Julian Ovenden, Robert Daws, Andy Wear, Stuart Wade, Anna Madeley, Wendy Craig and Linda Armstrong

programme.title: The Royal
programme.desc: New GP and anaesthetist Dr Jeffrey Goodwin has a bad start to the day when he witnesses a car crash, gets attacked by one of the drivers involved and has his vehicle and possessions stolen on his way to the Royal. Back at the hospital, Cheritons mother Annette arrives to collect her dead sons things, surprising staff nurse Meryl Taylor by revealing a photo of his fiancee. Rural medical drama, starring Paul Fox

Hospital drama, starring Kananu Kirimi and Robert Cavanah

programme.title: The Hotel Inspector
programme.desc: Alex Polizzi tries to reverse the fortunes of more hotels and BandBs around Britain, beginning with a visit to the Rosehill House Hotel in Burnley, Lancashire. Owned for the past 25 years by John and run by his daughter Emma, they have cluttered the 31-room establishment with tat and lost sight of who their customers are. Recently, bookings have taken a sharp decline and to make matters worse Johns wife Jacquie - once the life and soul of the hotel - has been forced to retire due to illness, leaving Emma to run the place alone

Also checking in today are two friends, with Michael treating his old pal JJ to a weekend away at their favourite holiday hotspot, and the pairs tickets arent the only thing he booked

programme.desc: John Torode prepares jerk chicken with rice and peas, while Lisa Faulkner makes a vegetarian curry full of paneer and potatoes

programme.title: Heathrow: Britains Busiest Airport
programme.desc: As the Covid-19 pandemic grips the nation, cameras follow life at Heathrow, as depleted staff members fight to keep the airport going. Customer service team member Demi is on hand to offer help to all those who are allowed to travel in and out of the airport, and has his work cut out as he tries to track down a missing passenger. Elsewhere, staff adjust to the new safety measures put in place to stop the spread of the virus

programme.title: Millionaire Hot Seat
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson hosts the quiz in which six hopefuls compete for £1 million, with only one player at a time able to take a place in the Hot Seat and answer multiple-choice questions to work the way up the Money Ladder. Each question is against the clock and if a player gets one wrong they are eliminated, the Top Prize drops down, and the next player takes their place

programme.title: Celebrity Sabotage
programme.desc: Prank show in which Joel Dommett, Judi Love, Sam Thompson and GK Barry work together to deceive unsuspecting members of the public by making them believe they are participating in new ITV shows. programme.title: Celebrity Catchphrase
