programme.title: News at 5.30
programme.desc: The latest national and international news stories from the Virgin Media News team

programme.title: Rookie Nurses
programme.desc: Wales youngest nurses continue to deal with all kinds of medical challenges thrown their way. In Carmarthen, theres no easing in for very newly qualified Caitlin who has to learn fast in her first week in AandE. Across the ward, her friend and colleague 22-year-old Mikey battles to keep a patient with severe hypothermia alive. On the Stroke Unit in Aberystwyth, a visit from Nelly the pet therapy dog lifts not just the patients spirits, but the nurses too, while nurse Gina helps keeps moral high on the ward with her funny banter

programme.title: Britains Got Talent
programme.desc: Ant and Dec host as the auditions return to Blackpools Winter Gardens, with more acts taking to the stage with performances to impress the crowd and judges Simon Cowell, Amanda Holden, Alesha Dixon and KSI. With the presenters yet to push their Golden Buzzer, might one of todays performers be just what the pair are looking for?

programme.title: The Chase Celebrity Special
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as Sunetra Sarker, Matty Lee, David Arnold and Basil Brush pit their wits against one of the ruthless Chasers in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They must work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.title: The 1% Club
programme.desc: Quiz game show hosted by Lee Mack in which the questions have nothing to do with remembering facts, but are all about logic and common sense. A hundred contestants begin every show, but who will make it to the end and answer a question only one per cent of the country can get right - and win up to £100,000?

programme.title: The Claudia Winkleman Show

programme.title: Who Wants to Be a Millionaire?

programme.title: Tour de Fred
programme.desc: Fred Sirieix is joined by his Olympic medal-winning diver daughter Andrea Spendolini-Sirieix to take on a series of cycle rides along the sometimes challenging but often spectacular roads of West Cork. The pair visit the butchers shop where black pudding was born, learn about the history of Barbary pirates, swap pedals for waves at a local surf school and share a seaside walk with alpacas

programme.title: The Hotel Inspector
programme.sub-title: The Sea Croft, Lytham St Annes
programme.desc: Alex Polizzi visits the Sea Croft, a struggling BandB in Lytham St Annes, Lancashire. Two years ago, owner Georgina gave up a career teaching business studies with a dream of running a successful guest house. Now, a £50,000 bill for unforeseen repairs and dismal occupancy has left her struggling to run the business. With her immaculate rooms lying empty, self-confessed neat freak Georgina cannot understand where she is going wrong. Alex can see the potential for success but getting Georgina to stop focusing on little details and start seeing the bigger picture does not prove easy

Drama, starring Jason Watkins

programme.title: Champions: Full Gallop

programme.title: Heathrow: Britains Busiest Airport
programme.desc: Documentary going behind the scenes of the airport, finding out how its infrastructure helps staff process thousands of passengers arriving from 84 countries. This time, the iconic Boeing 747 flies out of the airport for the final time, with plane spotters around the world hoping to bid it a fond farewell. Elsewhere, new cargo driver Dylan hopes his love for hypnosis can get him out of a jam

programme.title: Rookie Nurses
programme.desc: Wales youngest nurses continue to deal with all kinds of medical challenges thrown their way. In Carmarthen, theres no easing in for very newly qualified Caitlin who has to learn fast in her first week in AandE. Across the ward, her friend and colleague 22-year-old Mikey battles to keep a patient with severe hypothermia alive. On the Stroke Unit in Aberystwyth, a visit from Nelly the pet therapy dog lifts not just the patients spirits, but the nurses too, while nurse Gina helps keeps moral high on the ward with her funny banter

programme.title: The Chase Celebrity Special

programme.title: Soham: Catching a Killer

programme.title: Romesh Ranganathans Parents Evening

programme.title: Soham: Catching a Killer
programme.desc: Documentary focusing on the murders of Holly Wells and Jessica Chapman in 2002, detailing events from the desperate 14-day police search to the discovery of their bodies

programme.title: The Hotel Inspector
programme.desc: Alex Polizzi tries to reverse the fortunes of more hotels and BandBs around Britain, beginning with a visit to the Rosehill House Hotel in Burnley, Lancashire. Owned for the past 25 years by John and run by his daughter Emma, they have cluttered the 31-room establishment with tat and lost sight of who their customers are. Recently, bookings have taken a sharp decline and to make matters worse Johns wife Jacquie - once the life and soul of the hotel - has been forced to retire due to illness, leaving Emma to run the place alone

programme.title: Cash Trapped
programme.desc: Quiz hosted by Bradley Walsh in which six contestants battle it out across a series of fast-paced question rounds where the players must actively try to sabotage their opponents by beating them to the buzzer and trapping them out of the game. In the final round, the person who has earned the most cash will go head to head against the rest of the group to try to take their winnings home

programme.title: Hotel Benidorm: Fun-Loving Brits in the Sun
programme.desc: New receptionist Ruben is about to experience his first unhappy customer as he works his first solo shift, while groom-to-be Dwain is whisked away from his stag do to get wedding-ready with fitness instructor Sarah. programme.title: Ireland AM

programme.title: John and Lisas Weekend Kitchen

programme.title: Champions: Full Gallop
programme.desc: An insight into the tough reality of northern Jump racing, following several jockeys including three-time champion jockey Brian Hughes, Craig Nichol and Ross Chapman over the course of two days at Musselburghs Cheltenham Trials Weekend

programme.title: Heathrow: Britains Busiest Airport
programme.desc: As the Covid-19 pandemic grips the nation, cameras follow life at Heathrow, as depleted staff members fight to keep the airport going. Customer service team member Demi is on hand to offer help to all those who are allowed to travel in and out of the airport, and has his work cut out as he tries to track down a missing passenger. Elsewhere, staff adjust to the new safety measures put in place to stop the spread of the virus

programme.title: Millionaire Hot Seat
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson hosts the quiz in which six hopefuls compete for £1 million, with only one player at a time able to take a place in the Hot Seat and answer multiple-choice questions to work the way up the Money Ladder. programme.title: Celebrity Sabotage
programme.desc: Prank show in which Joel Dommett, Judi Love, Sam Thompson and GK Barry work together to deceive unsuspecting members of the public by making them believe they are participating in new ITV shows. programme.title: Celebrity Catchphrase

programme.title: The Hotel Inspector
programme.sub-title: The Sea Croft, Lytham St Annes
programme.desc: Alex Polizzi visits the Sea Croft, a struggling BandB in Lytham St Annes, Lancashire. Two years ago, owner Georgina gave up a career teaching business studies with a dream of running a successful guest house. Now, a £50,000 bill for unforeseen repairs and dismal occupancy has left her struggling to run the business. With her immaculate rooms lying empty, self-confessed neat freak Georgina cannot understand where she is going wrong. Alex can see the potential for success but getting Georgina to stop focusing on little details and start seeing the bigger picture does not prove easy

programme.title: Living with Lucy
programme.desc: Presenter Lucy Kennedy gets to know comedian Brendan Grace. As well as inviting her into his home, he takes her on a tour of some places that are special to him. She watches as he entertains crowds both north and south of the border, the pair travel to some of Irelands beauty spots and take in a cruise on the river Shannon.

programme.title: Cracker
programme.sub-title: To Say I Love You - Part Two
programme.desc: Part two of three. Fitzs life becomes more calamitous as he angers Penhaligon in a failed effort to make Judith jealous and turns a Gamblers Anonymous meeting into a card game. His only positive action is drawing up a profile of Sean and Tina, but the publicity it brings focuses the murderous couples anger on the police. Robbie Coltrane and Geraldine Somerville star, with Andrew Tiernan and Susan Lynch

programme.title: Vera, Farewell Pet
programme.desc: Documentary going behind the scenes to examine the appeal of ITV detective drama Vera as it comes to an end after 13 years. Brenda Blethyn reflects on her time as DCI Vera Stanhope, and talks about the camaraderie and joy of working on the show. Featuring interviews from key cast members - David Leon (DS Joe Ashworth), Jon Morrison (DC Kenny Lockhart), the directors, crew, and author Ann Cleeves, Veras creator

programme.title: Judge Rinders Crime Stories
programme.desc: Barrister Robert Rinder examines the case of Rolandas Poskus, a man who was found dead in a park in Hull, and Amanda Playle, a woman who was stalked by her ex-boyfriend

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: The Aidensfield police are puzzled when it seems a face from the past has become the unlikely victim of vandalism, and suspicions are soon aroused over the reasons for his sudden return to the village. Meanwhile, Vernon encounters dangerous wildlife as he stashes his capital in Ashfordly woods, but decides to turn the incident into another money-making scheme. Geoffrey Hughes stars

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Merton faces a race against time to stop a saboteur when Aidensfield hosts a prestigious car hill-climb. Meanwhile, Bernie finds an old car in a cowshed and decides to renovate it, giving Vernon his chance to scoop the winnings in the event. Rural police drama, starring Duncan Bell, Peter Benson and Geoffrey Hughes

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: A seemingly straightforward burglary investigation takes an unexpected turn when the victim is found to be leading a double life and suspicions are raised that the break-in could have been part of an insurance fraud. Blaketon falls for the charms of a solicitor and teams up with Ventress to help her track down the beneficiaries of an unusual bequest. Rural police drama, starring Derek Fowlds

programme.title: Agatha Christies Poirot
programme.desc: A murder gives rise to rumours of a communist conspiracy. Poirot becomes the centre of attention at the Chelsea Flower Show as he views a rose named in his honour, while Hastings receives a packet of seeds that give him hay fever. Whodunit, guest starring Anne Stallybrass (Heartbeat), with David Suchet

programme.title: Agatha Christies Poirot
programme.desc: A prominent bank manager is almost killed by a hit-and-run driver just before he is due to set sail on the luxury liner Queen Mary. Convinced his life is in danger, he persuades Poirot to accompany him - and his valuable shipment of liberty bonds - to New York. But the Belgian sleuths main concern is his own terrible sea-sickness. David Suchet stars

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Joe blackmails Robert and Aaron, Kim hires Ross, and Kerry is shocked

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Cain explodes with suppressed rage, Tracy is guilty, and Dawn questions Kim

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Charity is concerned, and when Cain has a fit of rage, she tries to reassure him

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Robert is accused, Joe is stunned, while Chas and Kim form an unlikely bond

programme.title: Dont Look Back in Anger
programme.desc: Archive news documentary looking back at the headline stories of the past 20 years, focusing this week on 2016

programme.title: Paul OGrady: For the Love of Dogs
programme.desc: The dog-loving funnyman pays another visit to Battersea, where he falls for the toothless boxer Prince. The pair hit it off immediately, leaving Paul to wonder whether he should rehome the canine himself. But there is bad news - Prince has a suspicious lump on his leg that requires urgent attention. Paul also meets Yorkshire terrier Dash, whose bark has lost its bite leaving him with a very unusual, squeaky voice. As if that were not bad enough, the little canine soon earns the nickname Dash the Slash after the comedian takes him out for a walk - and ends up with a wet leg

programme.title: Endeavour
programme.desc: Morse struggles to negotiate escalating social tensions as a protest at a hair salon exposes rapidly escalating racial unrest in the heart of the city. Meanwhile, a murder at an Army base threatens to unveil long-held secrets within the regiment. Guest starring Marcus Griffiths. Mystery, with Shaun Evans and Roger Allam

programme.title: Long Lost Family
programme.sub-title: Long Lost Family Special: The Unknown Soldiers
programme.desc: Davina McCall and Nicky Campbell join forces with investigators from the MODs Joint Casualty and Compassionate Centre to try to trace the families of nine First World War soldiers whose bodies were found together in a trench by roadworkers in Beselare, Belgium

programme.title: Hotel Benidorm: Fun-Loving Brits in the Sun
programme.desc: Documentary following life in the Spanish resort, this time at the Hotel Benidorm Plaza as Easter sees the start of the new holiday season. Checking in this week is a female rugby team from Manchester looking to let off some steam, a couple returning to relive their honeymoon, and friends hoping for solace in the sun

programme.title: The Secret Life of Our Pets
programme.desc: The second edition looks at the way pets use their intelligence to live in a human world, from a rabbit that can play basketball, to a rat that can learn how to drive and a tortoise that can solve a maze. The episode also demonstrates how the happier and more stimulated pets are, the smarter they become

programme.title: The Big Quiz
programme.sub-title: The Big Quiz: Coronation Street v Emmerdale
programme.desc: Stephen Mulhern hosts the annual quiz as cast members from Coronation Street and Emmerdale go head to head in rounds that test their knowledge of the past year in soap. Corrie team captain Jack P Shepherd (David Platt) is joined by Jodie Prenger (Glenda Shuttleworth), Sam Aston (Chesney Winter-Brown) and Georgia Taylor (Toyah Habeeb) to take on Emmerdales Lucy Pargeter (Chas Dingle), Bradley Riches (Lewis Barton), Shebz Miah (Kammy Hadiq) and captain Mark Charnock (Marlon Dingle)

programme.title: Grace
programme.desc: A brutal robbery at a secluded Brighton house pulls Grace and Branson into a challenging enquiry when thousands of pounds worth of antiques are stolen and the owner is left fighting for her life. The prime target of the incident appears to have been an item of great sentimental value to the homeowner and her brother and has deep emotional ties to a mystery from the 1960s involving their father. Return of the crime drama, starring John Simm

programme.title: Seconds to Spare (2002)
programme.desc: A former government agent is shocked to discover his arch-enemy has infiltrated a gang of extreme environmentalists - and is bent on contaminating the world with a deadly nerve gas unless hes paid a ransom. Thriller, starring Jerome Ehlers, Antonio Sabato Jnr, Kimberly Davies and Kate Beahan

programme.title: Chicago Fire
programme.desc: The entire team becomes trapped in a perilous situation at the county jail, and as the odds mount against the crew, unresolved issues come to a head for Dawson and Pete. Meanwhile, Shay remains hopeful about her plans to start a family, and Herrmanns wife Cindy goes into labour

programme.title: Judge Rinders Crime Stories
programme.desc: Barrister Robert Rinder tells the story of Frances Molloy, whose 18-year-old son was killed in a coach crash in 2012, leading her to fight for changes to the law, making it illegal for buses and coaches to use tyres that are more than 10 years old. He also examines the 1973 case of David McGreavy, who murdered three children he was supposed to be looking after

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: A girl disappears, prompting the police to begin a desperate search, convinced her errant father has snatched her. Blaketon decides to lend his expertise to the case - only to end up fighting for his life. Meanwhile, Crane and Bellamys dedication to duty sees them placing their lives on the line to rescue the youngster - but tragedy strikes. James Carlton, Mark Jordon and Derek Fowlds star

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: A new recruit reports for his first day of work at the police station, but given his familys history in Aidensfield, not all of the villagers are pleased to see him. Meanwhile, Ben and Lizs fund-raising efforts appear to have rubbed off on Vernon, who begins to show his charitable side

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: The residents of Aidensfield are terrorised by a blackmailer and Sergeant Merton is less than impressed by the investigative methods of the handsome newcomer PC Rob Walker. Meanwhile, Vernon embarks on a venture in the prestigious world of publishing, with the help of David and Bernie. Jonathan Kerrigan stars

programme.title: Agatha Christies Poirot
programme.desc: The Belgian sleuth is asked to vet potential suitors vying for the hand of an Australian heiress and discreetly accompanies her on a rail journey aboard the Plymouth Express, only to find himself investigating a jewel theft. Mystery, with David Suchet and Julian Wadham

programme.title: Agatha Christies Poirot
programme.desc: Poirot visits a garden fete where he meets the son of an old friend and his fiancee. After having tea with the couple, the sleuth suspects that they are both in danger and Hastings new interest in photography provides him with the clue to solve a mystery involving cyanide, failing brakes and mixed-up love lives.

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Theo ensures that Todd misses his marathon, Will refuses to join his family in condemning Megan, Adam suffers a dramatic loss of business, and Bernie and Dev find Mal waiting upon their return from holiday

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Theo tries a charm offensive on George and Christina, Kit panics when Mal names his attacker, Betsy and Dylan babysit Connie, and Hope warns Sam against listening to Daniel

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Theo stuns Todd by suggesting they get married right away, Bernie experiences divided loyalties to her son and husband, and Will struggles with being the focus of local gossip

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Bernie pays Mal a visit in his hotel room, George gains an insight into Theos treatment of Todd, Ben feels guilty for letting Will down, and Jodie offers Carla her childminding services

programme.title: Dont Look Back in Anger
programme.desc: Archive news documentary looking back at the headline stories of the past, focusing here on 2008

programme.title: Paul OGrady: For the Love of Dogs
programme.desc: Paul meets Happi, a Nepalese mountain dog - a breed never featured on the show before - who is a lover of vegetarian food, putting the funnymans culinary skills to the test as he prepares the poochs dinner. He also befriends Rumble the greyhound, who has an unusual problem - he is so happy, his tail has become damaged with all the wagging it does. Finally, Paul hears the story of Parsnip and Carrot, both suspected victims of backstreet breeding. Parsnip in particular is in a bad way, with infected mammary glands as a result of a recent pregnancy

programme.title: Endeavour
programme.desc: Morse investigates an assassination attempt at an international sporting event, but the case is very quickly closed. While Thursday is determined to walk away, Morse delves deeper, getting drawn into a web of intrigue involving espionage, big business and a dark secret in the heart of the Oxford community. Meanwhile, Thursday faces a moral dilemma as he tries to protect a woman from her violent husband

programme.title: Dont Look Back in Anger
programme.desc: Chronicling the highs and lows of 2025 and all the chaos in between

programme.title: The Mayfair Hotel Megabuild
programme.desc: As the construction work nears completion, the hotel turns its attention to the interiors, employing some of the worlds top designers and craftspeople to meet the expectations of its well-heeled guests. A highlight is expected to be the new penthouse, which will span the entire rooftop of Claridges and feature four bedrooms, a swimming pool and its own lake - as well as a rumoured £100,000 a night price tag

programme.title: Hairy Bikers: Route 66
programme.desc: Dave Myers and Si King mount their bikes for the final leg of their epic journey along the American road. They begin in Las Vegas, where they learn how to make apparently the best mashed potato in the world - inspiring them to cook a massive lobster and prawn cocktail. Back on the road they finally reach California, where they follow the path of the 49ers during the gold rush and learn about cooking with marijuana - which is perfectly legal in Los Angeles. They also enjoy the best tacos they have ever eaten at a taco festival, before reaching Santa Monica pier - the end of their trip

programme.title: Long Lost Family
programme.desc: The stories of two sons who were given up for adoption and the families who have stopped at nothing to find them. Andrew Barlow was adopted as a baby, but his birth mother Patricia Clark refused to sign the adoption papers and never gave up looking for him, while as a child, Julie Redman discovered that her mother Joan had given up her older brother for adoption

programme.title: Shetland
programme.desc: Calder receives some devastating news and Harry and Donnie worry as Tosh follows a controversial new theory. Intelligence from the Met puts Bobby Bain in a new light and Farida admits the truth behind Akmals death

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Todds friends discuss their evidence against Theo, Mal calls at Number 7 to deliver a bombshell to Dev, and Ollie urges Will to stop covering for Megan

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Cain is determined, while Matty delivers devastating news and Tracy is feeling heartbroken

programme.title: Anne
programme.desc: It is April 15, 1989 and Anne Williams says goodbye to her son as he heads to Hillsborough in Sheffield to watch Liverpool take on Nottingham Forest in the FA Cup semi-final, but she will never see him alive again. Fact-based drama about the woman who dedicated her life to campaigning for justice following her 15-year-old son Kevins death in the Hillsborough disaster. Maxine Peake stars

programme.title: Spies of Terror
programme.desc: The leads followed by Lucie and her team are not bearing fruit. Weakened by the Coalitions strikes, the Islamic State is suspected of preparing its last stand - a double attack on French soil. Spy thriller, starring Rachida Brakni, Fleur Geffrier, Vincent Elbaz and Pierre Perrier. In French

programme.title: Cold Feet
programme.desc: Karen is offered the job of editing Booker Prize-winning author Alex Welchs new novel, but cant decide whether to accept, until she claps eyes on him and realises hes handsome, charming and everything a man should be. Unfortunately, it isnt long before nanny Ramona overhears her reading a steamy passage from the manuscript aloud - and promptly gets hold of the wrong end of the stick. Hermione Norris and Denis Lawson star

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Geoff and Phil find two men acting suspiciously, but two others escape from the scene and the officers soon find themselves in danger and under siege. At the Aidensfield Arms the residents gather to watch the moon landing, although one guest gives Gina cause for concern

programme.title: Good Witch
programme.desc: Joys dream sends the three Merriwicks on a search for Phillip Harper, Joys father. Fantasy drama, starring Catherine Bell

programme.title: Impossible Engineering
programme.desc: How the Abrams Tank, the US Armys biggest land combat vehicle, is constructed, using access to the factory where they are built, as well as their testing grounds

programme.title: You and Me
programme.desc: Ben and Emma are both forced to weigh up their chances of happiness against the baggage of their pasts - as Ben hopes to confront Jess ex-lover Josh, and Emma deliberates over whether she can get married. Ben and Emma are both forced to weigh up their chances of happiness against the baggage of their pasts - as Ben hopes to confront Jess ex-lover Josh, and Emma deliberates over whether she can get married

programme.title: The Jonathan Ross Show: Special Guests
programme.desc: Another selection of the most memorable moments from recent series of the talk show. Among the guests featured are David Attenborough, Sheridan Smith, Kevin Hart, Michael Caine, Julie Walters, Hugh Jackman, Liam Gallagher, Michelle Keegan, Ed Sheeran, Tiffany Haddish and Robbie Williams.

programme.title: The Jonathan Ross Show: Special Guests
programme.desc: A final selection of the most memorable moments from recent series of the talk show. Among the guests featured are Michael McIntyre, Olivia Colman, David Beckham, Rebel Wilson, Jason Derulo, Sylvester Stallone, Michael B Jordan, Katherine Ryan, Will Ferrell, Demi Lovato, Sacha Baron Cohen, Ashley Banjo and Patrick Dempsey

programme.title: Unbeatable
programme.desc: Over three rounds, contestants vie to find the single unbeatable answer and streak ahead of the rest to make the final where they can win a cash prize. General knowledge quiz, hosted by Jason Manford

programme.title: The Chase Australia
programme.desc: Andrew OKeefe presents the Down Under version of the quiz show in which four people must work together to beat the Chaser and win a cash prize

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts the quiz in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents an hour of laughs, mistakes and outtakes from the quiz that were never meant to see the light of day. With contributions from chasers Anne Hegerty, Darragh Ennis, Mark Labbett, Jenny Ryan, Paul Sinha and Shaun Wallace

programme.title: UEFA Champions League Magazine Show
programme.desc: All the latest from the world of the UEFA Champions League, featuring highlights and interviews.

programme.title: Britains Brightest Family
programme.desc: Anne Hegerty hosts a quiz show in which 16 families take part in a knock out tournament as they attempt to win the holiday of a lifetime and the title of Britains Brightest Family. In the second episode, the Collier family from Cambridgeshire and the Brolly family from Co Antrim, Northern Ireland, battle for a place in the quarter-finals

programme.title: Dress to Impress
programme.desc: In Cardiff, three more singletons hope to win a date with their style savviness. Hot tub salesman Jack has no information to go on but the outfits that have been bought

programme.title: Yes Chef
programme.desc: Top chef Jun Tanaka has his eye on the prize as he puts three home cooks through a series of culinary challenges, including how to make the perfect red wine sauce

programme.title: Whos Doing the Dishes?
programme.desc: Former Big Brother winner Josie Gibson puts on a dinner party at her home as she plays mystery host to four strangers. Presented by Brian McFadden

programme.title: Cleaning Up Ireland
programme.desc: Tati and her team don hazmat gear to clean a filthy home overrun by pets. Window cleaners battle it out for employee of the month and Dublin Citys night crew scrub the streets

programme.title: In for a Penny
programme.desc: In this edition, Stephen Mulhern hosts the game show from York, setting members of the public unusual challenges for a chance to win anything from the price of their petrol to £1,000 in the big finale game

programme.title: Live Racing
programme.desc: Coverage of the Dubai World Cup, which comes from Meydan Racecourse. Florent Geroux rode Hit Show to victory in last years lucrative feature race

programme.title: Live International Football
programme.desc: Scotland v Japan (Kick-off 5.00pm). All the action from the friendly at Hampden Park. Steve Clarkes men begin preparations for their first FIFA World Cup finals since 1998 when they take on a side that have qualified for every tournament since then. The nations have not met since 2009, when two late goals gave Japan a 2-0 win in Yokohama, but Scotland will be hopeful of making home advantage count on this occasion after their excellent play-off win against Denmark here last time out

programme.title: Unbeatable
programme.desc: Over three rounds, contestants vie to find the single unbeatable answer and streak ahead of the rest to make the final where they can win a cash prize. General knowledge quiz, hosted by Jason Manford

programme.title: You Are What You Wear
programme.desc: Rylan Clark hosts the fashion makeover show, with volunteers including the husband-and-wife slimmer of the year champions, who are looking for outfits suitable for a glitzy, gold-themed ball, and 24-year-old Bournemouth woman Grace, who would like a wardrobe suitable for her new high-flying job. The stylists also get to choose over Jill, a 56-year-old raver who is looking for a style she can throw some serious shapes in, and Mel from Doncaster, who is keen to swap her all-weather obsession with flip flops and leggings for a sophisticated new look

programme.title: Side Hustlers
programme.desc: When the Hustlers host a marketing event to promote themselves and their business, the Investors drop in with a huge surprise for one shocked Hustler.

programme.title: Gogglebox Ireland
programme.desc: Deirdre OKane provides the voiceover as a selection of telly addicts pass comment on the weeks programmes from the comfort of their living rooms. From December 2016

programme.title: Dark Side of Reality TV
programme.desc: A behind-the-scenes look at the most prolific and iconic reality shows of the day - from Survivor to the Housewives franchises, Hells Kitchen and beyond. The Swan is supposed to be fairy-tale makeover television, but when the cast members discover theyre really contestants in a plastic surgery face-off, it becomes a gruelling nightmare

programme.title: Iain Stirlings CelebAbility
programme.desc: Iain Stirling is joined by Scarlett Moffat as she leads a group of three friends looking to win a slew of fabulous prizes by defeating four celebrities - Rachel Riley, James Lock, Amber Gill and Kae Kurd - in a series of silly games based on the unusual abilities the celebs believe they possess

programme.title: Snowpiercer
programme.desc: As the train detours from New Eden, an environmental catastrophe threatens everyone onboard

programme.title: Party Animals
programme.desc: Danny urges Jo to seek counselling to curb her escalating drink problem, but his attempts to help are thwarted when her husband runs off with the nanny. The candidates resort to dirty tactics as the race for Longton South hots up, but an innocent embrace with James could spell disaster for Ashikas campaign

programme.title: Tell Me Everything
programme.desc: Coming-of-age drama. Jonny has moved to a new town, made a fresh start at a new college and has a whole new group of friends, but will they help him attract the attention of fellow new student Bex? Meanwhile, Naisha is cramping best friend Dylans style and Ella is devastated when an exposing video goes viral

programme.title: Unbeatable
programme.desc: Over three rounds, contestants vie to find the single unbeatable answer and streak ahead of the rest to make the final where they can win a cash prize. General knowledge quiz, hosted by Jason Manford

programme.title: Sport Stories
programme.desc: An interview with the jockey, who reflects on a pioneering career, a life immersed in horse racing, and her role as a role model to other women

programme.title: Week in Review
programme.desc: Week In Review looks back at the main local, national and international stories of the week

programme.title: Whos Doing the Dishes?
programme.desc: Todays mystery host is football legend Peter Shilton. Will the diners tackle his cunning clues and walk off with the cash or can the famous goalie save himself from the dreaded dishes? Presented by Brian McFadden

programme.title: Yes Chef
programme.desc: The weekly final sees chefs Nigel Haworth, Amandine Chaignot, Paul Ainsworth and Jun Tanaka pair up with their chosen home cook partners and go head to head in the ultimate pro-am cooking competition. And to pile on the pressure, three-star Michelin chef Pierre Koffmann judges their final dishes. Culinary challenge, hosted by Sheree Murphy

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One with the Race Car Bed
programme.desc: Monica buys a bed from Janices husband but the wrong one is delivered. Joey gets a job teaching a soap opera acting class, while Rachel tries to ease the tension between Ross and her father

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One with the Giant Poking Device
programme.desc: Phoebe convinces herself she is a walking jinx who keeps causing people to die mysteriously. Monica hides the fact that Ben has been injured, while Chandler and Janice make a tough decision about their relationship

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: The gang holds an American football match on Thanksgiving. Opposing team captains Ross and Monica relive their sibling rivalry on the pitch, while Joey tries to outflank Chandler

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Rachel hands in her notice at Central Perk, while Ross accidentally breaks a girls leg and ends up having to go door to door peddling cookies. Joey starts a new job selling Christmas trees, but Phoebe thinks it is a cruel trade and sets about saving the merchandise

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One Where Chandler Cant Remember Which Sister
programme.desc: Rachels attractive and influential new friend sends Ross into a jealous frenzy, while Chandler gets drunk and falls for the curvaceous charms of one of Joeys many sisters - but later cannot remember which one

programme.title: In for a Penny
programme.desc: Stephen Mulhern hosts the game show from locations around the UK, setting members of the public unusual challenges for a chance to win anything from the price of their petrol, to £1000 in the big finale game. This week, Stephen is in Bristol

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents a compilation of out-takes, mistakes and funny moments from the quiz show, featuring regular Chasers Paul the Sinnerman Sinha, Jenny the Vixen Ryan, Anne the Governess Hegerty, Shaun the Dark Destroyer Wallace, Mark the Beast Labbett and Darragh the Menace Ennis

programme.title: Yes Chef
programme.desc: Theo Randall puts three amateur cooks through a series of culinary challenges, including how to make linguine asparagus carbonara, choosing one of them to join him in the final

programme.title: Inside MandS
programme.desc: Filmed over a year and with unprecedented access, for the first time, cameras have been invited inside every corner of MandS - from the build of a multi-million-pound store to top secret development kitchens at HQ. Store manager Dior prepares for the move to a new £15million shop, as well as the creation of a Percy Pig product the marketing team are hoping will rescue the little-known beauty department, and after years of fighting bad reviews and poor sales, the daring and bold new womens autumnwear range

programme.title: Posh Pawn
programme.desc: Prestige Pawnbrokers boss James Constantinou mixes business with pleasure as he heads to Spain to meet an entrepreneur friend whos looking to get a £100,000 loan on his luxury 18-metre yacht. A young widow finds it difficult to part with her late husbands vintage car to set up a new life in Croatia, jewellery expert Monika is shocked when shes asked to deal with a golden rat, and the shop staff struggle to put a price on some rare and problematic black coral statues

programme.title: Sam and Billie: Sister Act
programme.desc: Sam has a work crisis, Billie finally gets a health diagnosis, the sisters go off grid and reveal the extent of some childhood traumas, and Sue gets a makeover.

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: Julia Roberts talks about her latest role in psychological campus drama After the Hunt. Colin Farrell discusses playing a con man and gambling addict in atmospheric thriller Ballad of a Small Player. Singer Gloria Estefan introduces her new Spanish-language album Raices. And Benedict Cumberbatch talks about playing a newly widowed father in The Thing with Feathers. Plus, Robbie Williams performs Pretty Face from the forthcoming album BRITPOP

programme.title: The Claudia Winkleman Show
programme.desc: Jimmy Carr joins Claudia to talk about series two of Last One Laughing, Chase Infiniti speaks about her role in The Testaments - the sequel to The Handmaids Tale - and Lisa Kudrow talks about sitcom The Comeback. Meanwhile, Jamie Dornan previews Saturday Night Live UK, which he is guest hosting

programme.title: Piglets
programme.desc: A fresh intake arrives at Norbourne Police Training College, where the no-nonsense Superintendent Julie Spry and the less-strict Superintendent Bob Weekes have the job of overseeing the training of the new recruits. All of the rookies have motives for being there, but some are more legitimate than others. Comedy set in a police training college, starring Sarah Parish and Mark Heap

programme.title: Party Animals
programme.desc: Jos career takes a further blow when her husband sells a kiss-and-tell story to the tabloids, leaving Danny and Kirsty to get her back on track. Rivals Ashika and Scott grow closer as the election in Sedley intensifies, but when a CD containing photos of her and James is anonymously sent to Scott, he faces an impossible decision

programme.title: Skin AandE
programme.desc: Life within a specialist clinic, where a team of top dermatologists treats a range of skin conditions for patients who often havent been able to get treatment on the NHS

programme.title: Unbeatable
programme.desc: Over three rounds, contestants Neil, Malcolm, Emma and Nicola vie to find the single unbeatable answer and streak ahead of the rest to make the final where they can win a cash prize. General knowledge quiz, hosted by Jason Manford

The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as Phil from Liverpool, Helen from Bedford, Jamie from Edinburgh and Victoria from Warwick take on one of the Chasers and hope to win a cash prize. They work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught
